text: Might the HDD platters be used in some role akin to gauge blocks? They are manufactured with some extremely tight tolerances...

author: @coondogtheman
text: In order to get more sound out of this you'll need a real amp and not just the output from your mac. I made one and kept the entire unit but removed the heads and bent the top head so it doesn't touch the disk and make that squeaking noise. I removed the top cover over the coil because the magnet was on the bottom and stuck this toy magnet thing that has a windmill on it and it seemed to work well and sounded pretty decent and was adjustable.

author: @colleenforrest7936
text: Does the foam cutter cut builders board?

author: @bogdanvasut8915
text: Some time ago I converted an old phone earpiece to a speaker. I used a sheet of paper folded lengthwise as a fan and I glued the ends together to form a circular fan. I attached a wire in the center and soldered it to the armature of the earpiece. The sound is decent though faint, but strong enough to listen to the local AM station in a diode only receiver.

author: @jasonwitt8619
text: I was almost going to say this was rerun day but then it hit me, the one from last week was using a microwave oven transformer, nice twist in using the hard drive in showing sound. Now you got me thinking how to make a microphone that we can use without a computer and run it straight into the hard drive speaker, now that would be totally awesome. It would be kind of like making our own karaoke device out of things lying around, lol just brilliant

author: @tisstuart
text:
I don't know about you but I really enjoy final contributions of outdated electronics. On that cue, my mind reminds me that capacitors can whistle once when they make popcorn. A circuit of individually controlled diodes is called a distribution circuit. Which by chance, is software managed and hardware based. So when the old Intel x86 architecture was met with fate and calendar dates. It was fun to change the pinouts inside the pc case, add a jumper wire or more, and put in a floppy disk that made the speakers play midi music while the program (virus) went about making popcorn and whistled in que.  A bit of smoke and a breaker reset on the back and we have destruction ala 1999. SkyNet fears me. hahahahahaha     .... ha 
I digress the effect varies by available voltage\amperage.  Still fun though.
